dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I thought gathering evidence would be the hard part. I was wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They found out we're trying to {quest_action}. They sent soldiers to {location} to intimidate us. {target_npc} was taken in for questioning. They confiscated half the supplies you gathered, calling it 'back taxes.' I need you to retrieve more {quest_item} and find a way to deliver our evidence to the regional council. There's an old messenger trail {location_hint}, but {enemy_type} have been spotted along it.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_threat} is becoming dangerous. The only thing worse than a liar is a liar with soldiers.

playerResponses.text: Intimidation won't work when the truth gets out. I'll find a way through.
Soldiers, monsters, and a race against time. Just another delivery job.
They have soldiers and we have documents. This fight is over.

dialogueChunks.intro: They had the nerve to come back here, {player_name}. Right through the front door, smiling.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} returned acting as if nothing happened. But I found new ledgers hidden in the back room: {quest_focus} is still being undermined. The contraband trail leads to a warehouse {location_hint}. I need you to document what you find: crates, manifests, anything that proves the scope of the betrayal. And I need more {quest_item} to keep things running while I prepare my case.
dialogueChunks.outro: They looked me in the eye and lied. Again. {quest_stakes} unless we stop this at the council table.

playerResponses.text: The warehouse will tell the whole story. I'll go.
Coming back and lying to your face. That takes a special kind of rotten. I'll get your evidence.
Partner disputes should stay between partners. I'm not a detective.

dialogueChunks.intro: The guild master moved faster than we expected, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} has been moved from the guild hall. {target_npc} watched guards carry sealed chests out the back door. They're destroying evidence. Without the original signed covenant, it's my word against theirs. There's a clerk who kept copies in a private archive {location_hint}. The {enemy_type} enforcers are watching the roads, but if you can reach that archive, we have an airtight case.
dialogueChunks.outro: Destroying a signed covenant is a crime in itself. {quest_threat}, but they're getting desperate.

playerResponses.text: Destroying evidence means they're scared. I'll find the copy.
Enforcers on the roads and evidence vanishing. We need to move fast.
If they're destroying records, they've already won. Walk away.
